entries.name: 3 Game-Changing Rules for FTMO Payouts!
entries.channelName: Paradigm Shift Trading | Prop Firm Reviews
entries.description:
FTMO Payout. 3 Rules. No Guesswork.

When it comes to prop firm success, simplicity beats complexity. Here are the 3 golden rules we follow to secure payouts on accounts like FTMO:

✅ Rule 1: Never risk more than 1% per trade – this keeps your account safe and your mindset strong.
✅ Rule 2: Stick to at least 1:3 risk-to-reward ratio – small risk, big reward, always.
✅ Rule 3: Never revenge trade – emotions are your enemy, discipline is your weapon.

By applying these rules, we turned a $200K FTMO account into $23K in profit. No gambling. No random entries. Just strategy, discipline, and execution.

🔥 Want to learn how to automate this process or pass your next challenge stress-free? DM us now and let’s get you funded!

entries.description:
The complaint alleges that the defendants, operating under the name "My Forex Funds," enticed retail customers with the promise of becoming "professional traders" using Traders Global's capital for trading against third-party "liquidity providers" and sharing in trading profits. They reassured customers that "your success is our business" and claimed they only profited when customers did. However, the reality is that Traders Global, not third-party "liquidity providers," acted as the counterparty for most customer trades.

► For Business Enquiries mail  : tenzin.jamtson@jamtsonfx.com

The complaint further asserts that Traders Global actively employed tactics to reduce the likelihood of customer profitability. entries.description:
Other people strive for fame;
I avoid the limelight,
preferring to be left alone.
Indeed, I seem like an idiot:
no mind, no worries.

I drift like a wave on the ocean.
I blow as aimless at the wind.

All men settle down in their grooves;
I alone am stubborn and remain outside.
But wherein I am most different from others is
in knowing to take sustenance from the great Mother!

entries.name: Funded Accounts is a Scam ? 🤯 | Trade with Purab
entries.description:
Are funded accounts a scam? Many influencers make it sound like an easy way to trade big with little money, but the reality is very different. In this video, I break down the truth about funded accounts and why you need to be cautious.

📉 What You’ll Learn in This Video:
1️⃣ Funded accounts claim to offer $5,000 trading accounts for just a $50 fee, with promises of earning 50-80% of the profits.
2️⃣ The reality: More than 95% of people fail to make consistent profits, and many end up losing their money.
3️⃣ Beware of scams – not all brokers are trustworthy. If your broker isn’t reliable, you might never see the profits you earn.
4️⃣ The funded account model is like an exam where only 2-3% pass, and even then, there’s no guarantee of payouts.

🚨 Key Takeaway: Always verify the trustworthiness of your broker before committing to a funded account. Stay informed and trade smart.

how to clear prop firm challenge
entries.description:
📓 Trading Journal - Day 70

Market was bullish since morning, and I was waiting for it to reach my resistance level. This was a 4H resistance, so as price started approaching the zone, I took a sell trade with 0.5 lots.
But this was an early entry, so my stop loss had to be bigger, placed just above the zone.

My second trade was taken in the same zone — this time it was exactly at my resistance level, the perfect entry I was waiting for.
The first position was still open, and this second trade had a smaller stop loss since it was right at the zone.

entries.name: Everything you need to know about funded accounts🚀📈
entries.description:
🚨Funded trading accounts offer a pathway for you to trade Forex without risking their own capital.
Typically, traders undergo an testing process by prop firms, showcasing their skills and strategies. Upon passing, the firm provides a funded account with capital. The evaluation often includes performance metrics like consistency, risk management, and profitability. It's a mutually beneficial arrangement; traders gain access to capital, while firms seek profitable traders to share in their success. This model makes trading accessible for young traders, allowing skilled people to participate in financial markets with reduced personal risk.

In this video, I break down the Topstep XFA payout rule change released on December 30, 2025, and explain exactly how it impacts new Topstep Express Funded Accounts going forward.

This update does NOT affect your first payout and does NOT apply to Live Funded Accounts. It applies only to the second payout and beyond for traders who were not Topstep funded before the rule change.

I also review real Topstep trader statistics, including why in 2024 only ~3.5% of traders reached a payout, and only ~0.5% made it to Live Funded — and how this new rule is designed to address that reality.

Topstep Express Funded Account – Updated Payout Policy

To request a payout from a Topstep Express Funded Account, you must meet both requirements:

1️⃣ Five (5) Winning Trading Days

• A winning day = $150 or more Net P&L
• The days do not need to be consecutive

2️⃣ Be Profitable Since Your Last Payout

• Any profit qualifies — even $0.01
• This requirement does NOT apply to your first payout

entries.name: Pass TopStep’s 50% Consistency Rule: Daily $3K Target Strategy for Prop-Firm Success
entries.description:
📚 Grab my FREE e-books on prop-firm success: https://propfirmkid.activehosted.com/f/1

💬 Join our VIP Discord for live setups & AI-driven prop-firm intel: https://discord.gg/Uxv4Wm9CCz

Prop firms like TopStep use consistency rules to limit daily profits—ensuring longer market exposure reduces your odds of passing. In this video, I break down exactly how to turn that rule into your advantage:

What Is a Consistency Rule?

A cap on daily profit (e.g., TopStep’s 50% rule means you can’t earn more than half your total target in one day).

Daily Target Calculation

For a $100K challenge with a $6K profit goal, divide by 0.5 → $3K/day max.

Aggressive Profit Plan

Risk 1.5% per trade at 1:2 R/R to hit the $3K daily target.

Minimizes time in the market while fully using your allowed consistency buffer.

Why It Works

Shorter exposure = fewer opportunities for drawdowns or rule breaches.

Focused daily goals keep your strategy disciplined and scalable.

Action Steps

Calculate your firm’s

entries.name: 💹 5 Forex Brokers in UAE 2024 | Choosing a Forex Broker in Dubai 2024
tags: find a forex broker in dubai 2024
find a good broker in dubai 2024
forex trading legal hai dubai mein
forex trading is legal in dubai 2024
how to do forex trading in dubai 2024
find best forex brokers in dubai 2024
entries.description:
A growing number of international Forex brokers have opened subsidiaries in Dubai, the financial center of the Middle East and the fastest-growing Forex market.

One of the first choices anyone located in Dubai must make when looking for a new broker is whether to go for one which is located and regulated in Dubai or not. Forex brokers regulated in Dubai operate under the oversight of the Dubai Financial Services Authority (DFSA). While the DFSA has a reputation as a light regulator, it ensures a highly competitive as well as relatively secure trading environment for Forex trading in Dubai. 

It also serves as one of the best jurisdictions for Forex brokers to use as a gateway to the MEASA region, which represents one of the fastest-growing sectors for financial services. Therefore, when you see a Forex broker setting up in Dubai, you usually see one that wants to expand, and as such may be focusing on making a very competitive offering to clients.

It is a simple forex ftmo challenge strategy with an account site of 10K but like all my  strategies it is based on market structure. To display the latest lows and highs in the chart I use the Highest High Lowest Low Strategy indicator on tradingview and for a second confirmation for entries and exits the Williams %R indicator is added.

I explain the ftmo strategy step by step and show the 3 random trades that I took. After that I add the trades to the backtester TraderEdge to see the winrate and profit in percent. You fnd the app here:
https://www.traderedge.app/

Here you can find my TradingView indicators on this page:
https://www.tradingview.com/u/trading-guide/#published-scripts

entries.name: Apex Trader Funding Eval Ban Explained What Actually Happens How to Keep Trading & Getting Funded
entries.description:
Got hit with an Apex eval ban or "evaluation block" email? You're not alone—thousands of traders are searching "Apex eval ban" right now, thinking it's game over or that Apex is a scam. But here's the REAL truth: It's NOT a full ban, shutdown, or scam. Apex isn't banning you forever—it's restricting new evaluation purchases while letting you KEEP your existing Performance Accounts (PAs), continue trading, withdraw profits, and even scale up to 20 funded accounts.  In this video (Trade With JesseW), I break down:  Why Apex is doing eval bans/blocks in 2026 (rules, reviews for live trading shift, consistency checks, etc.)

entries.name: Apex Trader Funding Fees: Monthly vs. Lifetime
tags: hacks to passing apex trader fundung
how to pass a funded account
entries.description:
Apex Trader Funding offers two fee options for funded accounts: monthly and lifetime. Monthly fee: The monthly fee is a recurring payment that you make each month to keep your funded account active. The fee amount varies depending on the size of your account.

Lifetime fee: The lifetime fee is a one-time payment that you make to purchase your funded account outright. The fee amount is higher than the monthly fee, but it can save you money in the long run if you plan on keeping your funded account for more than a few months.

Which fee option is right for you?

The best fee option for you will depend on your individual circumstances. If you are not sure how long you plan on keeping your funded account, the monthly fee may be a good option. If you are sure that you will be trading with Apex Trader Funding for the long term, the lifetime fee may be a better value.
#proptrading #daytrading

entries.description:
How to create a funded account with City Traders Imperium prop firm.

1. Have the cost of the account in your crypto wallet in USDT plus $4 for network fee.

3. click pay with crypto

entries.name: How I Passed A 1 Million Dollar Challenge: LUX FIRM SCAM's 0.03% Hidden Risk = Not for Swing Traders
entries.description:
OBSERVATION:
(1) LUX Trading Firm gives you only $3000 total risk on a 1 million dollar account, but this is not so explicit in the rules, and it is very confusing.
(2) LUX is a terrible decision for Long-term Swing Traders and there is a better solution.
(3) It is a deceptive marketing ploy: You can only risk 0.3% or $3000 from the dangling "1 million dollars" that is supposedly offered to manage.  This is the same as trading 300,000 USD and risking 1% on a trade like other prop firms.  So, it is not really 1 million dollars, just a marketing ploy and calculation manipulation.

CONCLUSION:
Lux Firm's non-explicit risk of 0.3% of the highest realized account balance is not a firm for long-term swing traders, but only for day traders that traders intraday. With long-term swing trading, it will take you about 2 to 3 years to pass LUX's Million Dollar Challenge.  If you have $5000 - $30,000 USD of you own to spare, you are better off investing in US stocks yourself for 2 to 3 years. Your

entries.name: Summer brings payout issues for simulated prop firms - will they survive?
entries.description:
This summer, we're witnessing a significant shakeup in the prop trading industry. On June 15th, Funded Engineer, the first influencer-launched prop firm, announced it would cease operations due to industry upheavals, including issues with My Forex Funds, MetaQuotes, and FPFX. They're filing for bankruptcy, marking the end of an era.

ForexPropReviews also removed Forex Prop Firm and FundingYourTrades from their site, citing "lack of professionalism" and payout issues. Meanwhile, Discord discussions reveal payout problems with Finotive-Funding, highlighting the increasing instability among prop firms this season.

entries.name: Are PROP FIRMS even LEGAL?!
entries.description:
Hey traders! The recent prop firm drama has the whole trading world buzzing. Shutdowns, scandals, and rumors... is the online prop firm industry legit, or about to implode? Let's dive into it!

The Legal Loophole: Online prop firms exist in a gray area. It's the lack of regulation, not the concept itself, that's inviting shady practices.
The Challenge Scam: Those unrealistic trading challenges and profit splits? Many firms are just pyramid schemes in disguise, and regulators are taking notice.
Old-School vs. New: Traditional prop firms provided real capital – the crazy challenges are a mostly online invention.
The Future of Regulation: Incoming rules will clean things up. Expect the get-rich-quick schemes to disappear, leaving proven firms with real trading accounts in the spotlight.
The Legit Way: Look for firms, like Lux Trading Firm, that focus on trader development and real account growth, not fake challenges.

entries.name: Limited Budget Challenge - £5 for 5 Days - DAY 4
entries.description:
This is day 4 of an exercise in which I set myself the task of cooking meals for 5 days on a total budget of £5 
As usual, there will be rules and permissions - this time though, no foraging, no scavenging, no freebies.

Day 1 (containing the rules and parameters) is here: https://youtu.be/WHDTP8P9SFI

It's important to note: this is NOT an attempt to 'play at being poor' (although it always does make me appreciate things that I normally take for granted), NEITHER is it an attempt to 'prove that people on benefits could get by with less' (in fact if anything, it proves the opposite - because this was a difficult thing to do - that's why it's called a 'challenge').
Primarily, this is an exercise that I set myself specifically to try to provoke a creative response; to nurture or develop transferrable skills of creativity and adaptability.

entries.name: Bulenox Review: Best Terms Worst Accountability
entries.description:
The Bulanox infrastructure utilizes a 90% profit split and a 100% initial threshold to solve the "trader retention" problem, yet operates under an anonymous leadership structure that lacks the public accountability found in named-executive competitors like Tradeify. While the platform offers direct live capital routing to solve the "simulation lag" issue, this advantage is negated by a vaguely defined flipping rule that has been documented to withhold over $10,000 in earned profits through post-hoc trade analysis. Contrastingly, the tier-two competitor provides a more rigorous 10-day evaluation period and educational integration to solve the "long-term consistency" gap, offering a documented resolution path that Bulanox's D+ Business Standards Bureau rating currently lacks.

What is forex and how to make money 
The most basic forms of forex trades are a long trade and a short trade. In a long trade, the trader is betting that the currency price will increase in the future and they can profit from it. A short trade consists of a bet that the currency pair’s price will decrease in the future. 

A scalp trade consists of positions held for seconds or minutes at most, and the profit amounts are restricted in terms of the number of pips. Such trades are supposed to be cumulative, meaning that small profits made in each individual trade add up to a tidy amount at the end of a day or time period. 

Day trades are short-term trades in which positions are held and liquidated in the

how to clear prop firm challenge
entries.description:
📓 Trading Journal - Day 72

The market came to the 4H resistance level, but I should have waited for some confirmation. There was no confirmation at all, yet I still took a sell-side trade.
Confirmation is the second thing — the main thing is momentum. It’s important to know where the actual momentum is, which basically means the direction of the market. It’s very important to know where the market is headed, because if your direction is right, you’ve already won the big game.

🎯 Some days it’s just about staying disciplined and sticking to your plan — losses are part of the process.

entries.name: I Became A Trader For 24 Hours!
What is Prop Trading? | In the Money by Zerodha
entries.description:
In this episode, we break down one of the most misunderstood corners of Indian markets — Proprietary Trading (Prop Trading).

I’m your host, Sandeep Rao (SEBI Registered Research Analyst – INH000013059).

Prop trading is often spoken about as a shortcut to high leverage, fast money, and institutional-style trading. In reality, much of what circulates under the “prop trading” label in India today is either misunderstood, misrepresented, or outright illegal.

This episode is a deep dive into what prop trading is actually supposed to be, how it evolved in India, how legitimate firms operate, and how recent scams misused the concept to lure traders with unrealistic leverage and false promises.

Using real regulatory context, historical examples, and recent events, we separate legit proprietary trading from the shadow ecosystems that exist around it.

🧭 Chapters:
00:00 – Intro: The ₹150 crore ‘prop trading’ scam
02:00 – The origin story: Jobbers, brokers & early prop trading
02:44 – Harsha

entries.description:

What is Prop Trading?  http://www.financial-spread-betting.com/Spread-trading-faqs.html  PLEASE LIKE AND SHARE THIS VIDEO SO WE CAN DO MORE!  Proprietary trading (also referred to as "prop trading") happens traders trade with the company's own money as opposed to depositors' money, so as to make a profit for itself.  Proprietary trading firms business is finding good traders and funding them so that they trade and make money for the firm.  A proprietary trading firm will usually put limits or caps on its traders so as to limit the risk.  They will interview traders and do their own due diligence but will also cap the risk with some restrictions and limitations on account size.  If you start making money for a prop firm they will start giving you more

entries.name: How to Update Tax info and Payout Settings on Facebook Content Monetization Beta? #tax #info #update
entries.description:
📢 𝗠𝗔𝗛𝗔𝗟𝗔𝗚𝗔𝗡𝗚 𝗔𝗟𝗔𝗟𝗔!
BAGO KA MAKATANGGAP NG PAYOUT MULA SA FACEBOOK, SIGURADUHIN MONG AYOS ANG IYONG TAX INFORMATION! 💸📝

Maraming content creators at page owners ang na-hold ang kanilang Facebook payout ngayong 2025 dahil sa kulang o outdated na tax information. Sayang ang pinaghirapang earnings kung hindi ito maaayos agad! Pero don’t worry—madali lang itong gawin at kayang-kaya mo! 💡

🔍 Narito ang Step-by-Step Guide para I-Setup o I-Update ang Iyong Tax Info:

1️⃣ Mag-login sa Facebook Creator Studio o sa Professional Dashboard
2️⃣ I-click ang Monetization, tapos piliin ang Payout Settings
3️⃣ I-click ang View/Edit Payout Account
4️⃣ Sa ibaba ng page, hanapin ang Tax Information section
5️⃣ Piliin ang Update o Add Tax Info
6️⃣ Sagutin ang mga tanong base sa iyong status:
➤ Individual o Business ba ang account mo?
➤ Filipino citizen ka ba? May U.S. income ka ba?
7️⃣ Kung ikaw ay Non-U.S. creator, fill-out ang W-8BEN Form
8️⃣ I-submit at hintayin ang confirmation na

entries.name: Is Prop Trading a SCAM? 😳 The Truth You Need to Hear
entries.description:
Is prop trading a scam? 🤔
Short answer: No — but only if you choose the right firm.

🚨 Watch out for:
❌ Instant funding promises
❌ Super easy “challenges”
❌ Zero structure or risk management

These firms often sound good, but they’re built to fail you.
The legit “G” firms? They’ve got solid evaluations, real risk rules, and long-term vision. 📊

If it sounds too good to be true… yeah, you know the rest.
Do your homework before handing over your hard-earned cash 💸

entries.description:
Tradeify says they’ll fund your trading dreams—but after reviewing their rules, accounts, and trader feedback, the story’s more complicated. Here’s what you need to know: They offer 3 account types:

Advanced
Growth
Straight-to-Sim

With funding up to $750K. They advertise bi-weekly payouts in 24–48 hours—but real traders say the experience doesn’t match the promise.

Key issues:

Trailing intraday drawdown on Advanced accounts can blow your evaluation even after a recovery.
Straight-to-Sim offers soft limits, but no real-time dashboard.
Some traders report 24-hour data lag—making it impossible to manage risk accurately.

Reddit users say: “I’ve tried 4 accounts… something feels off with their data.” Tradeify looks sharp, but strict backend rules and data delays raise serious red flags. Subscribe now — because not all prop firms are what they seem.

Back2Funded cost: $599 × 4 = $2,396

Time = money, and skipping a Combine cycle saved me weeks of grind + rebuild time.

entries.name: How Losers Pass Prop Firm Challenges (The Funded Trader, True Forex Funds, FTMO)
tags: how to pass prop firm challenge
entries.description:
A 100% win rate is a false expectation. Some of the greatest traders in the industry have explained that they are profitable, funded, and maintaining consistent payouts with a 50-65% win rate. The art of losing is a critical skill in trading. I have found that when I accepted the fact that I would lose and designed a risk plan that accommodated for losses, I saw much more success as a trader. You can get funded with The Funded Trader, My Forex Funds, and True Forex Funds by learning the average win rate of your strategy and learning how to continue to trade every time you see your confirmations. As we grow as traders we can increase our win rate through several factors, but there is no escaping gaining the skill of losing.

Leave a comment and let me know what you think?

City Traders Imperium Review & Discount code

CTI is a proprietary firm based in the UK, London. CTI offers day trading, standard, classic, and direct funding programs. Classic and standard funding programs are one-step evaluation programs, while direct funding allows traders to skip the evaluation and start earning from the start. On the other hand, the day trading funding program is a two-step evaluation. 

All of the mentioned funding programs also offer unique scaling plans that allow you to acquire capital up to $2,000,000 per account. CTI allows you to trade on MetaTrader 5 while using a 3rd party program as their broker.

entries.name: The Boys Season 5 Review: Episodes 1-7
entries.description:
The Boys couldn’t have returned at a more fitting time. The series directly speaks to the harried political climate of 2026 with its focus on rising fascism and the ultimate power and subsequent fragility of hope. Thematically, the series has all its ducks in a row, and there are many great character moments to dig into over the course of the first seven episodes of Season 5. However, the show is hardly flawless as it makes its long-awaited return. The plot is sluggish at best, with the early episodes restoring a more traditional status quo and later episodes taking their sweet time in building to a dramatic showdown. And along the way, not every character enjoys as much spotlight time as they deserve. Still, The Boys Season 5 is a lot of fun even when it proves less than laser-focused.

Check out our full review of the first seven episodes of The Boys Season 5. The final season's first two episodes will premiere on Prime Video on April 8, 2026.

The Boys is based on the comics by Gart

entries.name: THE BOYS - Season 5 - Episode 1 & 2 - Spoiler Review
entries.description:
The Boys are back in town, for a 2 Episode Season Premiere! The FINAL Season kicks off with such a perfect blend of Old School Vaught hijinks seasons 1 and 2 vibes meets seasons 3 and 4's escalation. The first episode here, I think is their strongest season debut to date, which is saying a lot! Homelander feels full of menace in a way only Antony Starr can bring. It's wonderful to see all of THE BOYS back together as a team, and a new member of The Seven joins, directly into that number 2 spot. All while the show... beautifully mirrors the world and tries to find new ways to separate itself from being an (elevated with Superheroes)... documentary. Lots of thoughts! Full Spoilers for these two eps! Join me! And let me know your own thoughts below!

entries.name: Day 5 New Year Challenge: Cardio Walk & Strength | 40 minutes, 2500 steps | Seniors, beginners
entries.description:
Day 5 of the 7-day New Year Challenge is a 40 minute workout that includes a cardio walk with our second strength workout for the week! The walk combined with the warmups and cooldowns will give you 25 minutes of cardio, racking up about 2500 steps! Up the challenge by increasing tempo and/or intensity. The 15-minute strength session includes exercises for your arms, legs, and core.

Timestamps:
00:00  Workout Overview
01:45  Warm Up
04:15  Cardio Walk
24:16  Strength Training
38:12  Cooldown

At 15:28, we talk about a recent study that noted middle-age people who walked at least 7,000 steps a day on average were 50 percent to 70 percent less likely to die of any cause over the next decade, compared with those who took fewer steps. Here is a link to a news report about the study: https://nbcnews.to/3F6BQvd

Also, remember to look out for the Secret Word for a chance to win yes2next gear with our New Year Challenge Sweepstakes! The Grand Prize is a yes2next sweatshirt and water bottle.

entries.name: The 5 Whys of Problem-Solving Method
entries.description:
To learn about the 5 Whys method, see the article at:
https://www.mindtools.com/a3mi00v/5-whys?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=video&utm_campaign=fivewhys&utm_content=description

Problems that simply won't go away, no matter what you try, are often symptoms of deeper issues.

Applying a "quick fix" to such problems may be convenient, but it rarely addresses those deeper issues.

The 5 Whys technique is a simple but powerful tool for getting to the root of a problem so you can solve it properly.

Find out more about the 5 Whys problem-solving technique by watching the video.
